description_long: This bungalow features a traditional stilted design with a long, rectangular main structure elevated on numerous wooden posts. The roof is steeply pitched and thatched, extending over the main living area and two prominent gabled end sections. The exterior walls are clad in dark wood, with decorative wooden railings and latticework visible on the porch and entryway. A wide veranda runs along one side, accessible by a staircase, and is partially enclosed with ornate wooden panels. Windows are arranged in a linear pattern along the main facade, interspersed with wooden shutters and decorative elements. The overall appearance suggests a Southeast Asian or vernacular tropical architectural influence, emphasizing natural materials and elevated construction for ventilation and flood protection.
description: A stilted, wooden bungalow with a steep thatched roof, wide veranda, and decorative railings, featuring a long rectangular form and gabled ends.
description_view_0: This view highlights the main entry porch with ornate railings and the gabled end section, showing the elevated stilt construction.
description_view_1: From above, the full length of the thatched roof and both gabled ends are visible, emphasizing the elongated structure and stilt supports.
description_view_2: This angle shows the opposite gabled end and the open veranda area, with the staircase and decorative latticework clearly visible.
description_view_3: The view focuses on the long facade with its series of windows, the main porch, and the detailed wooden railings along the veranda.
attribute_window_arrangement: linear series of windows along main facade with decorative shutters
